message.content:
Audit the popcrm-web frontend for dead links, stub actions, and broken backend wiring. I need to know which UI interactions actually persist to the backend vs. which are no-ops or local-only.

Read these files in full:
- /worksp/popcrm-web/src/features/crm/api.ts
- /worksp/popcrm-web/src/features/crm/CrmDataContext.tsx
- /worksp/popcrm-web/src/features/crm/components/OpportunityModal.tsx (just the composer/send section)
- /worksp/popcrm-web/src/features/crm/components/NoteDrawer.tsx
- /worksp/popcrm-web/src/features/crm/components/TaskDrawer.tsx
- /worksp/popcrm-web/src/features/crm/components/EmailDrawer.tsx
- /worksp/popcrm-web/src/features/crm/components/ApprovalDrawer.tsx
- /worksp/popcrm-web/src/features/crm/pages/EmailRoutingPage.tsx (ignore rules section)
- /worksp/popcrm-web/src/features/crm/pages/SettingsPage.tsx

For each UI action that writes data (button clicks, form submits, status changes), report:
1. Whether it calls an API function
2. Whether that API function exists in api.ts
3. Whether it only updates local state (optimistic with no real persist) or actually hits the backend

Also check: are there any buttons/links in the UI that have onClick handlers that are clearly stubs (empty functions, just toast calls with no API, navigate to non-existent routes)?

Report format: a flat list of findings, each one: [WIRED / STUB / LOCAL-ONLY] — component/action — one sentence explanation. Under 400 words total.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll audit the frontend for dead links, stub actions, and broken backend wiring. Let me read these files systematically.

content.content:
1	import { createItem, readItems, updateItem } from '@directus/sdk'
2	import { directus } from '@/lib/directus'
3	import type {
4	  CrmAiModelConfig,
5	  Buyer,
6	  CrmDepartment,
7	  CrmEmailMessage,
8	  CrmIgnoreRule,
9	  CrmLicensorApprovalThread,
10	  CrmMeetingNote,
11	  CrmNote,
12	  CrmOpportunity,
13	  CrmTask,
14	  Retailer,
15	} from '@/lib/types'
16	
17	export async function fetchOpportunities(): Promise<CrmOpportunity[]> {
18	  return directus.request(
19	    readItems('crm_opportunity', {
20	      fields: [
21	        'id',
22	        'name',
23	        'amount',
24	        'close_date',
25	        'stage',
26	        'program_type',
27	        'season_year',
28	        'division',
29	        'production_po_number',
30	        'sales_order_number',
31	        'hard_delivery_date',
32	        'ai_summary',
33	        'ai_state',
34	        { retailer: ['id', 'name', 'customer_status'] },
35	        { contact: ['id', 'name', 'email'] },
36	        { department: ['id', 'name'] },
37	        { factory: ['id', 'name'] },
38	        { project: ['id', 'title'] },
39	      ],
40	      sort: ['stage', 'name'],
41	      limit: -1,
42	    }),
43	  ) as Promise<CrmOpportunity[]>
44	}
45	
46	export async function setOpportunityStage(id: string, stage: string) {
47	  return directus.request(updateItem('crm_opportunity', id, { stage }))
48	}
49	
50	export async function updateOpportunity(id: string, values: Partial<CrmOpportunity>) {
51	  return directus.request(updateItem('crm_opportunity', id, values as never))
52	}
53	
54	export async function fetchRetailers(limit = 300): Promise<Retailer[]> {
55	  return directus.request(
56	    readItems('retailer', {
57	      fields: ['id', 'name', 'domain', 'customer_status', 'chain_type', 'routing_aliases'],
58	      sort: ['name'],
59	      limit,
60	    }),
61	  ) as Promise<Retailer[]>
62	}
63	
64	export async function updateRetailer(id: string, values: Partial<Retailer>) {
65	  return directus.request(updateItem('retailer', id, values as never))
66	}
67	
68	export async function fetchBuyers(limit = 300): Promise<Buyer[]> {
69	  return directus.request(
70	    readItems('buyer', {
71	      fields: [
72	        'id',
73	        'name',
74	        'email',
75	        'phone',
76	        'job_title',
77	        'contact_type',
78	        'scope',
79	        { retailer: ['id', 'name'] },
80	        { department: ['id', 'name'] },
81	      ],
82	      sort: ['name'],
83	      limit,
84	    }),
85	  ) as Promise<Buyer[]>
86	}
87	
88	export async function updateBuyer(id: string, values: Partial<Buyer>) {
89	  return directus.request(updateItem('buyer', id, values as never))
90	}
91	
92	export async function fetchDepartments(limit = -1): Promise<CrmDepartment[]> {
93	  return directus.request(
94	    readItems('crm_department', {
95	      fields: [
96	        'id',
97	        'name',
98	        'category',
99	        'division',
100	        'active',
101	        { retailer: ['id', 'name'] },
102	        { primary_buyer: ['id', 'name', 'email'] },
103	      ],
104	      sort: ['name'],
105	      limit,
106	    }),
107	  ) as Promise<CrmDepartment[]>
108	}
109	
110	export async function fetchEmailMessages(limit = 300): Promise<CrmEmailMessage[]> {
111	  return directus.request(
112	    readItems('crm_email_message', {
113	      fields: [
114	        'id',
115	        'subject',
116	        'sender',
117	        'recipients',
118	        'received_at',
119	        'routing_status',
120	        'routing_method',
121	        'body_preview',
122	        { retailer: ['id', 'name'] },
123	        { department: ['id', 'name'] },
124	        { opportunity: ['id', 'name', 'stage'] },
125	      ],
126	      sort: ['-received_at'],
127	      limit,
128	    }),
129	  ) as Promise<CrmEmailMessage[]>
130	}
131	
132	export async function updateEmailMessage(id: string, values: Partial<CrmEmailMessage>) {
133	  return directus.request(updateItem('crm_email_message', id, values as never))
134	}
135	
136	export async function fetchMeetingNotes(limit = 100): Promise<CrmMeetingNote[]> {
137	  return directus.request(
138	    readItems('crm_meeting_note', {
139	      fields: [
140	        'id',
141	        'name',
142	        'date',
143	        'participants',
144	        'summary',
145	        'action_items',
146	        'source',
147	        'fireflies_transcript_id',
148	        { retailer: ['id', 'name'] },
149	        { department: ['id', 'name'] },
150	        { contact: ['id', 'name', 'email'] },
151	      ],
152	      sort: ['-date'],
153	      limit,
154	    }),
155	  ) as Promise<CrmMeetingNote[]>
156	}
157	
158	export async function fetchIgnoreRules(): Promise<CrmIgnoreRule[]> {
159	  return directus.request(
160	    readItems('crm_ignore_rule', {
161	      fields: ['id', 'name', 'pattern', 'match_type', 'emails_skipped'],
162	      sort: ['name'],
163	      limit: -1,
164	    }),
165	  ) as Promise<CrmIgnoreRule[]>
166	}
167	
168	export async function createIgnoreRule(values: Partial<CrmIgnoreRule>) {
169	  return directus.request(createItem('crm_ignore_rule', values as never)) as Promise<CrmIgnoreRule>
170	}
171	
172	export async function fetchAiModelConfigs(): Promise<CrmAiModelConfig[]> {
173	  return directus.request(
174	    readItems('crm_ai_model_config', {
175	      fields: ['id', 'name', 'email_routing_model', 'fireflies_routing_model', 'transcript_split_model', 'opportunity_summary_model'],
176	      sort: ['name'],
177	      limit: -1,
178	    }),
179	  ) as Promise<CrmAiModelConfig[]>
180	}
181	
182	export async function updateAiModelConfig(id: string, values: Partial<CrmAiModelConfig>) {
183	  return directus.request(updateItem('crm_ai_model_config', id, values as never))
184	}
185	
186	export async function fetchNotes(limit = 200): Promise<CrmNote[]> {
187	  return directus.request(
188	    readItems('crm_note', {
189	      fields: [
190	        'id',
191	        'title',
192	        'body',
193	        'action_items',
194	        'source',
195	        'fireflies_transcript_id',
196	        { retailer: ['id', 'name'] },
197	        { contact: ['id', 'name', 'email'] },
198	        { opportunity: ['id', 'name', 'stage'] },
199	        { department: ['id', 'name'] },
200	      ],
201	      sort: ['-id'],
202	      limit,
203	    }),
204	  ) as Promise<CrmNote[]>
205	}
206	
207	export async function createNote(values: Partial<CrmNote>) {
208	  return directus.request(createItem('crm_note', values as never)) as Promise<CrmNote>
209	}
210	
211	export async function updateNote(id: string, values: Partial<CrmNote>) {
212	  return directus.request(updateItem('crm_note', id, values as never))
213	}
214	
215	export async function fetchTasks(limit = 200): Promise<CrmTask[]> {
216	  return directus.request(
217	    readItems('crm_task', {
218	      fields: [
219	        'id',
220	        'title',
221	        'body',
222	        'status',
223	        'due_at',
224	        { retailer: ['id', 'name'] },
225	        { contact: ['id', 'name', 'email'] },
226	        { opportunity: ['id', 'name', 'stage'] },
227	        { department: ['id', 'name'] },
228	        { assignee: ['id', 'first_name', 'last_name', 'email'] },
229	      ],
230	      sort: ['status', 'due_at'],
231	      limit,
232	    }),
233	  ) as Promise<CrmTask[]>
234	}
235	
236	export async function createTask(values: Partial<CrmTask>) {
237	  return directus.request(createItem('crm_task', values as never)) as Promise<CrmTask>
238	}
239	
240	export async function updateTask(id: string, values: Partial<CrmTask>) {
241	  return directus.request(updateItem('crm_task', id, values as never))
242	}
243	
244	export async function fetchApprovalThreads(limit = 200): Promise<CrmLicensorApprovalThread[]> {
245	  return directus.request(
246	    readItems('crm_licensor_approval_thread', {
247	      fields: ['id', 'name', 'property_name', 'stage', 'submitted_date', 'response_date', 'due_date', 'licensor_comments', { opportunity: ['id', 'name', 'stage'] }],
248	      sort: ['stage', '-submitted_date'],
249	      limit,
250	    }),
251	  ) as Promise<CrmLicensorApprovalThread[]>
252	}
253	
254	export async function updateApprovalThread(id: string, values: Partial<CrmLicensorApprovalThread>) {
255	  return directus.request(updateItem('crm_licensor_approval_thread', id, values as never))
256	}
257

1	import {
2	  createContext,
3	  useCallback,
4	  useContext,
5	  useEffect,
6	  useMemo,
7	  useState,
8	  type Dispatch,
9	  type ReactNode,
10	  type SetStateAction,
11	} from 'react'
12	import {
13	  fetchAiModelConfigs,
14	  fetchApprovalThreads,
15	  fetchBuyers,
16	  fetchDepartments,
17	  fetchEmailMessages,
18	  fetchIgnoreRules,
19	  fetchMeetingNotes,
20	  fetchNotes,
21	  fetchOpportunities,
22	  fetchRetailers,
23	  fetchTasks,
24	} from './api'
25	import { FIREFLIES_HEALTH_URL, isApprovalResolved, needsRouting } from './constants'
26	import type {
27	  Buyer,
28	  CrmAiModelConfig,
29	  CrmDepartment,
30	  CrmEmailMessage,
31	  CrmIgnoreRule,
32	  CrmLicensorApprovalThread,
33	  CrmMeetingNote,
34	  CrmNote,
35	  CrmOpportunity,
36	  CrmTask,
37	  Retailer,
38	} from '@/lib/types'
39	
40	export interface CrmDashboardStats {
41	  accounts: number
42	  contacts: number
43	  openOpportunities: number
44	  emails: number
45	  needsRouting: number
46	  routed: number
47	  skipped: number
48	  companyOnly: number
49	  companyDept: number
50	  meetings: number
51	  openTasks: number
52	  pendingApprovals: number
53	}
54	
55	interface CrmDataValue {
56	  loading: boolean
57	  error: boolean
58	  refresh: () => void
59	  firefliesOk: boolean | null
60	
61	  opportunities: CrmOpportunity[]
62	  retailers: Retailer[]
63	  buyers: Buyer[]
64	  departments: CrmDepartment[]
65	  emails: CrmEmailMessage[]
66	  meetings: CrmMeetingNote[]
67	  ignoreRules: CrmIgnoreRule[]
68	  aiConfigs: CrmAiModelConfig[]
69	  notes: CrmNote[]
70	  tasks: CrmTask[]
71	  approvals: CrmLicensorApprovalThread[]
72	
73	  setOpportunities: Dispatch<SetStateAction<CrmOpportunity[]>>
74	  setRetailers: Dispatch<SetStateAction<Retailer[]>>
75	  setBuyers: Dispatch<SetStateAction<Buyer[]>>
76	  setEmails: Dispatch<SetStateAction<CrmEmailMessage[]>>
77	  setMeetings: Dispatch<SetStateAction<CrmMeetingNote[]>>
78	  setIgnoreRules: Dispatch<SetStateAction<CrmIgnoreRule[]>>
79	  setAiConfigs: Dispatch<SetStateAction<CrmAiModelConfig[]>>
80	  setNotes: Dispatch<SetStateAction<CrmNote[]>>
81	  setTasks: Dispatch<SetStateAction<CrmTask[]>>
82	  setApprovals: Dispatch<SetStateAction<CrmLicensorApprovalThread[]>>
83	
84	  stats: CrmDashboardStats
85	}
86	
87	const CrmDataContext = createContext<CrmDataValue | null>(null)
88	
89	const OPEN_TASK = (t: CrmTask) => t.status !== 'DONE' && t.status !== 'CANCELED'
90	const PENDING_APPROVAL = (a: CrmLicensorApprovalThread) => !isApprovalResolved(a.stage)
91	
92	export function CrmDataProvider({ children }: { children: ReactNode }) {
93	  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true)
94	  const [error, setError] = useState(false)
95	  const [firefliesOk, setFirefliesOk] = useState<boolean | null>(null)
96	
97	  const [opportunities, setOpportunities] = useState<CrmOpportunity[]>([])
98	  const [retailers, setRetailers] = useState<Retailer[]>([])
99	  const [buyers, setBuyers] = useState<Buyer[]>([])
100	  const [departments, setDepartments] = useState<CrmDepartment[]>([])
101	  const [emails, setEmails] = useState<CrmEmailMessage[]>([])
102	  const [meetings, setMeetings] = useState<CrmMeetingNote[]>([])
103	  const [ignoreRules, setIgnoreRules] = useState<CrmIgnoreRule[]>([])
104	  const [aiConfigs, setAiConfigs] = useState<CrmAiModelConfig[]>([])
105	  const [notes, setNotes] = useState<CrmNote[]>([])
106	  const [tasks, setTasks] = useState<CrmTask[]>([])
107	  const [approvals, setApprovals] = useState<CrmLicensorApprovalThread[]>([])
108	
109	  const load = useCallback(async () => {
110	    setLoading(true)
111	    setError(false)
112	
113	    // Load every collection independently. One collection being forbidden
114	    // (e.g. a Directus permission gap) or failing must NOT blank the whole app —
115	    // that section just stays empty. We only surface a hard error if everything
116	    // fails (auth/network down).
117	    const sources: Array<{ name: string; run: () => Promise<unknown>; set: (v: never) => void }> = [
118	      { name: 'opportunities', run: fetchOpportunities, set: setOpportunities as (v: never) => void },
119	      { name: 'accounts', run: () => fetchRetailers(-1), set: setRetailers as (v: never) => void },
120	      { name: 'contacts', run: () => fetchBuyers(-1), set: setBuyers as (v: never) => void },
121	      { name: 'departments', run: () => fetchDepartments(), set: setDepartments as (v: never) => void },
122	      { name: 'emails', run: () => fetchEmailMessages(-1), set: setEmails as (v: never) => void },
123	      { name: 'meetings', run: () => fetchMeetingNotes(-1), set: setMeetings as (v: never) => void },
124	      { name: 'ignore rules', run: fetchIgnoreRules, set: setIgnoreRules as (v: never) => void },
125	      { name: 'AI config', run: fetchAiModelConfigs, set: setAiConfigs as (v: never) => void },
126	      { name: 'notes', run: fetchNotes, set: setNotes as (v: never) => void },
127	      { name: 'tasks', run: fetchTasks, set: setTasks as (v: never) => void },
128	      { name: 'approvals', run: fetchApprovalThreads, set: setApprovals as (v: never) => void },
129	    ]
130	
131	    const results = await Promise.allSettled(sources.map((s) => s.run()))
132	    const failed: string[] = []
133	    results.forEach((result, i) => {
134	      if (result.status === 'fulfilled') {
135	        sources[i].set(result.value as never)
136	      } else {
137	        failed.push(sources[i].name)
138	        sources[i].set([] as never)
139	      }
140	    })
141	
142	    if (failed.length) {
143	      console.warn(`CRM: could not load ${failed.join(', ')} (continuing with the rest)`)
144	    }
145	    // Hard error only when nothing loaded at all.
146	    setError(failed.length === sources.length)
147	    setLoading(false)
148	  }, [])
149	
150	  useEffect(() => {
151	    queueMicrotask(() => void load())
152	    queueMicrotask(() => {
153	      fetch(FIREFLIES_HEALTH_URL)
154	        .then((res) => setFirefliesOk(res.ok))
155	        .catch(() => setFirefliesOk(false))
156	    })
157	  }, [load])
158	
159	  const stats = useMemo<CrmDashboardStats>(() => {
160	    const by = (status: string) => emails.filter((e) => e.routing_status === status).length
161	    return {
162	      accounts: retailers.length,
163	      contacts: buyers.length,
164	      openOpportunities: opportunities.filter((o) => o.stage !== 'CLOSED').length,
165	      emails: emails.length,
166	      needsRouting: emails.filter((e) => needsRouting(e.routing_status)).length,
167	      routed: by('ROUTED'),
168	      skipped: by('SKIPPED'),
169	      companyOnly: by('COMPANY_ONLY'),
170	      companyDept: by('COMPANY_DEPT'),
171	      meetings: meetings.length,
172	      openTasks: tasks.filter(OPEN_TASK).length,
173	      pendingApprovals: approvals.filter(PENDING_APPROVAL).length,
174	    }
175	  }, [retailers, buyers, opportunities, emails, meetings, tasks, approvals])
176	
177	  const value: CrmDataValue = {
178	    loading,
179	    error,
180	    refresh: () => void load(),
181	    firefliesOk,
182	    opportunities,
183	    retailers,
184	    buyers,
185	    departments,
186	    emails,
187	    meetings,
188	    ignoreRules,
189	    aiConfigs,
190	    notes,
191	    tasks,
192	    approvals,
193	    setOpportunities,
194	    setRetailers,
195	    setBuyers,
196	    setEmails,
197	    setMeetings,
198	    setIgnoreRules,
199	    setAiConfigs,
200	    setNotes,
201	    setTasks,
202	    setApprovals,
203	    stats,
204	  }
205	
206	  return <CrmDataContext.Provider value={value}>{children}</CrmDataContext.Provider>
207	}
208	
209	export function useCrmData() {
210	  const ctx = useContext(CrmDataContext)
211	  if (!ctx) throw new Error('useCrmData must be used within CrmDataProvider')
212	  return ctx
213	}
214

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive audit report.

## Audit Report: Dead Links, Stubs, and Backend Wiring

**STUB — OpportunityModal composer send button — onClick only clears text without calling any API or persisting comment; pressing Enter has identical behavior.**

**LOCAL-ONLY — OpportunityModal stage change — Updates local state optimistically, then awaits setOpportunityStage() API call; correctly wires to backend via updateItem('crm_opportunity').**

**WIRED — NoteDrawer save — Calls updateNote() which exists in api.ts and persists via updateItem('crm_note') to Directus.**

**WIRED — TaskDrawer status change — Calls updateTask() which exists in api.ts and persists via updateItem('crm_task').**

**WIRED — EmailDrawer routing save — Calls updateEmailMessage() which exists and persists routing_status/routing_method/retailer/department/opportunity via updateItem('crm_email_message').**

**WIRED — EmailDrawer create ignore rule — Calls createIgnoreRule() which exists and persists to backend via createItem('crm_ignore_rule').**

**WIRED — ApprovalDrawer stage save — Calls updateApprovalThread() which exists in api.ts and persists via updateItem('crm_licensor_approval_thread').**

**WIRED — EmailRoutingPage ignore rules add — Calls createIgnoreRule() with pattern, match_type; correctly wires to backend.**

**WIRED — SettingsPage AI model field change — Calls updateAiModelConfig() which exists and persists via updateItem('crm_ai_model_config').**

**Critical finding: The OpportunityModal composer is non-functional.** Line 417 clears composerText on Enter, and line 423 clears it on button click—but neither action calls any API to persist the comment. No createComment() function exists in api.ts. This is a broken feature: users type, press send, the UI clears, but nothing hits the backend.
